title: Spennera indecora
taxonomy: Spennera indecora is classified in the family Sapindaceae. This family belongs to the order Sapindales. The Sapindales order is a part of the larger clade called Rosids. Rosids are a subset of the larger clade called Eudicots. Eudicots are a group of flowering plants that make up the majority of the angiosperm species.
uses: Spennera indecora is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine practices. It is used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The plant's extracts are also believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. In some regions, Spennera indecora is used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal benefits.

constituents: Spennera indecora contains active constituents that are primarily responsible for its medicinal properties. The main constituents of Spennera indecora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. Flavonoids present in the plant have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Alkaloids in Spennera indecora are known for their analgesic and antispasmodic effects. Furthermore, saponins in the plant have been found to possess antimicrobial and anticancer properties.
parts: Spennera indecora, also known as the Australian bloodwood or Spenner's bloodwood, is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine. The plant's bark is considered to be one of its medicinal parts, which has been used to treat various health issues. The bark of Spennera indecora contains tannins and other compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditional healers have also used the plant's leaves and roots in medicinal preparations. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Spennera indecora in modern medicine.
